seoTitle: How to Edit and Customize Task Status in ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to rename, reorder, and add custom task statuses in ClickUp so your pipeline stages match your team's actual workflow.
intro: Default status names like 'To Do' and 'In Progress' don't always match how your team actually talks about work. This guide covers renaming existing statuses, adding brand-new stages, and reordering them to reflect your real delivery pipeline, whether that's for an entire Space or just one specific List.

steps.heading: Open Space Settings
steps.body: Hover over a target Space in your sidebar, click the three-dot settings icon, and select Space Settings.

steps.heading: Go to Task Statuses
steps.body: Inside Space Settings, open the Task Statuses panel to view your current pipeline stages.

steps.heading: Rename an existing status
steps.body: Hover over a status row and click the pencil icon to safely rename it, such as changing 'To Do' to 'Backlog'.

steps.heading: Add a new status
steps.body: Click '+ Add Status' to inject a completely new stage into your workflow, useful for tracking review or QA steps.

steps.heading: Reorder your statuses
steps.body: Use the drag handles on the left side of each row to arrange statuses into the correct chronological sequence.

steps.heading: Save and apply overrides at the List level
steps.body: Click Save Changes to update the Space, or navigate into a specific List to apply a distinct local status override.

faq.question: Will renaming a status affect existing tasks?
faq.answer: No, renaming a status just updates the label; tasks already in that stage stay there under the new name.

faq.question: Can one List have different statuses than the rest of the Space?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply a local status override to a specific List to give it its own unique tracking structure.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a status that tasks are using?
faq.answer: You'll typically be prompted to move those tasks to another status before the old one can be fully removed or archived.

faq.question: Do status changes update the Kanban board automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once you save your changes, the Kanban board view reflects the updated columns immediately.
